llm.reason: Proper noun - Aotea is a place name (Great Barrier Island / Aotea Square)
Proper noun - Te Arawa is a tribal/canoe name
Proper noun / ethnonym - refers to the Maori people specifically
Proper noun prefix - Ngāti is used exclusively as a tribal prefix (e.g. Ngāti Porou)
Proper noun - Orewa is a town north of Auckland
Proper noun - Otane is a town in Hawke's Bay
English loanword - not a native Te Reo Maori word
Proper noun - Timor is an island/country name, not a Maori word
Ambiguous - could be the country Tonga; as a common word (south) it should be 'tonga' but risks confusion
Proper noun - Te Urewera is a region/former national park
